radiology_order.exam: CT ABD & PELVIS W & W/O CONTRAST, ADDL SECTIONS
UNILAT UP EXT VEINS US RIGHT

steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS W & W/O CONTRAST, ADDL SECTIONS)

ensemble.other_hypothesis: Post-procedural complications from PTBD placement and ampullary dilation (e.g., biloma, hematoma, pneumoperitoneum, drain malposition, hepatic injury, retroperitoneal bleed); biliary ductal pathology extent/progression; chest pain etiology (inferior sternal, cardiac vs. subphrenic/diaphragmatic irritation)
ensemble.information_gap: Whether the recent percutaneous transhepatic biliary drain placement and ampullary dilation have caused any immediate post-procedural complications (bleeding, biloma, perforation, drain malposition) and the anatomic status of the biliary system and surrounding structures
ensemble.expected_finding: Presence or absence of peri-hepatic hematoma, biloma, pneumoperitoneum, retroperitoneal hemorrhage, or drain malposition; also assessment of biliary ductal dilation extent and any new intra-abdominal pathology explaining the post-op epigastric pain and inferior sternal chest pain
ensemble.appropriateness_reason: The patient was just admitted directly after a complex interventional biliary procedure (PTBD placement, ampullary dilation, brushings/biopsies) and has new post-procedural epigastric pain and chest pain with a drain in place, making cross-sectional imaging essential to evaluate for procedural complications that cannot be assessed by labs or exam alone.
grounding: History: 'she had successful ampullary dilatation and L PTBD. Admitted directly to ___'
History: 'c/o post-op epigastric pain but denies n/v. denies f/c. +c/o chest pain inferior aspect of sternum'
Physical examination: '+epigastric ttp without r/g, drain in place'
PTC report: 'Two brushings were performed, followed by two forceps biopsies of the left biliary system. The ampulla was then dilated with 8mm x 40mm balloon.'
PTC report: 'Diffuse, moderate bilateral intrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. Mild irregularity of the left duct.'
PTC report: 'An 8.5 ___ Cook multipurpose biliary catheter was advanced under fluoroscopic guidance. The pigtail was formed.'
Laboratory tests: 'Lipase: 30.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-60.0)' — normal, making pancreatitis less likely
Laboratory tests: 'Troponin T: <0.01' — cardiac cause of chest pain less likely but not fully excluded
ensemble.reasoning: This patient was admitted directly after a technically complex PTBD placement with ampullary dilation and biliary biopsies, and now has new epigastric tenderness and inferior sternal chest pain with a drain in situ. The primary concern is a post-procedural complication — such as hepatic or peri-hepatic hematoma, biloma, pneumoperitoneum, or drain malposition — none of which can be reliably excluded by physical exam or the available labs. A CT abdomen and pelvis with and without contrast provides the most comprehensive cross-sectional survey to identify or exclude these complications, characterize the biliary anatomy relative to the drain, and explain the new symptoms.

The primary concern is a post-procedural complication — such as hepatic or peri-hepatic hematoma, biloma, pneumoperitoneum, or drain malposition — none of which can be reliably excluded by physical exam or the available labs. vindication.actual_finding: The percutaneous biliary drain is appropriately positioned terminating in the second portion of the duodenum with no hematoma, biloma, free air, free fluid, or drain malposition identified, though mild fat stranding anterior to the pancreatic head and a small abdominal wall seroma were noted.
vindication.relation_to_expectation: The report directly addressed all major expected findings — ruling out peri-hepatic hematoma, biloma, pneumoperitoneum, and retroperitoneal hemorrhage, confirming drain positioning, and identifying minor incidental findings (fat stranding, seroma) that may partially explain the post-procedural pain without indicating a serious complication.
